title: Demo 2: Discovery in Action
description: Run the server and hit A2A endpoints from the terminal. See the protocol live — no SDK, just HTTP.

scenes.title: Introduction
scenes.description: Discovery is not optional — it's fundamental.

scenes.title: The Discovery Flow
scenes.description: Show the HTTP sequence before we do it live.

scenes.title: Start the SpecialistAgent
scenes.description: Launch the agent host in a terminal.

scenes.title: Fetch the well-known AgentCard
scenes.description: GET /.well-known/agent-card.json — the first thing any A2A client does.

scenes.title: Fetch all agents
scenes.description: GET /agents — discover the full catalog of a multi-agent host.

scenes.title: Look at the test file
scenes.description: Open the requests.http file to see what a JSON-RPC message looks like.

scenes.title: Send a raw JSON-RPC message
scenes.description: Talk to the pirate agent using nothing but curl.

moves.command: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5100/a2a/pirate -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":"1","method":"SendMessage","params":{"message":{"role":"user","messageId":"m1","parts":[{"kind":"text","text":"Ahoy matey!"}]}}}' | jq .

scenes.title: Talk to the DictionaryAgent (zero AI)
scenes.description: Same protocol, no AI behind it — just a lookup table.

moves.command: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5100/a2a/dictionary -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":"2","method":"SendMessage","params":{"message":{"role":"user","messageId":"m2","parts":[{"kind":"text","text":"What is A2A?"}]}}}' | jq .

scenes.title: Takeaway
scenes.description: You just spoke A2A — no SDK needed.
